title: Power Supply Board Failure
shortDescription: The standby LED blinks twice repeatedly, indicating a power supply board malfunction.
fullDescription:
When the Sony Bravia TV standby indicator blinks red twice in a repeating cycle, it points to a failure in the power supply board. The power supply is responsible for converting AC mains voltage into the various DC voltages required by the TV's main board, backlight driver, and other components.

This error commonly occurs after a power surge, lightning strike, or gradual capacitor degradation over time. Swollen or leaking electrolytic capacitors on the power board are the most frequent root cause. In some cases, a faulty voltage regulator IC or blown fuse on the power board can also trigger this code.

Before replacing any parts, try unplugging the TV for 60 seconds to perform a full power reset. If the blinking persists, the power supply board will need inspection and likely replacement.

causes.cause: Swollen or leaking capacitors on the power supply board
Power surge or lightning damage to the power board
Faulty voltage regulator on the power supply board
Blown fuse on the power supply board

symptoms: Standby LED blinks red twice in a repeating pattern
TV will not power on
Clicking sound from inside the TV when attempting to turn on
TV turns off immediately after powering on

fixSteps.title: Perform a power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV from the wall outlet and wait 60 seconds. Press and hold the power button on the TV (not the remote) for 30 seconds to drain residual power. Plug the TV back in and try turning it on.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the power supply board
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the back panel of the TV by unscrewing all Phillips screws around the perimeter. Locate the power supply board (the board connected to the AC power cord). Visually inspect for swollen, bulging, or leaking electrolytic capacitors. Check for burn marks or discoloration on the board.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty capacitors or the power board
fixSteps.instruction: If swollen capacitors are found, desolder them and replace with capacitors of identical specifications (voltage, capacitance, temperature rating). Alternatively, replace the entire power supply board with a compatible replacement. Reconnect all ribbon cables and harnesses, reinstall the back panel, and test.

whenToCallPro: If you are not comfortable working with mains voltage components or soldering on circuit boards, contact a Sony authorized repair center or a qualified TV repair technician.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV 2 blinks red light fix power supply

faq.q: Is it safe to open my Sony TV to inspect the power board?
faq.a: Yes, but always unplug the TV and wait at least 30 minutes before opening. Capacitors on the power board can hold a charge. Do not touch any components while the TV is plugged in.

faq.q: How much does a Sony TV power supply board cost?
faq.a: Replacement power supply boards typically cost between $40 and $120 depending on the model. Professional repair including labor ranges from $150 to $350.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause the 2-blink error?
faq.a: Yes, power surges are one of the most common causes. Using a surge protector can prevent future damage.

title: Backlight Inverter or LED Driver Failure
shortDescription: The standby LED blinks four times repeatedly, indicating a backlight or LED driver board failure.
fullDescription:
A four-blink pattern on the Sony Bravia standby LED indicates a problem with the backlight system. On older CCFL-backlit models, this points to a failed inverter board. On newer LED-backlit models, it indicates a failure in the LED driver board or the LED backlight strips themselves.

The backlight system provides illumination behind the LCD panel. When the driver circuit detects an overload, short circuit, or open circuit in the backlight array, it shuts down and the TV enters a protection mode indicated by the 4-blink code.

Common causes include failed LED strips where individual LEDs have burned out, a faulty LED driver board, or damaged cable connections between the driver board and the LED strips. On older models, the CCFL tubes or inverter board transformer may have failed.

causes.cause: Failed LED backlight strips with burned-out LEDs
Defective LED driver board
Damaged ribbon cable between LED driver and backlight strips
Failed CCFL inverter board (older models)

symptoms: Standby LED blinks red four times in a repeating pattern
TV appears to turn on but screen is completely black
Faint image visible when shining a flashlight at the screen
TV shuts off immediately after the Sony logo appears
Audio plays but no picture

fixSteps.title: Perform the flashlight test
fixSteps.instruction: Turn on the TV in a dark room. Shine a bright flashlight directly at the screen at a slight angle. If you can see a faint image, the backlight has failed but the panel and main board are working. This confirms a backlight or driver issue.

fixSteps.title: Power reset the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV from the wall outlet for 60 seconds. Press and hold the power button on the TV for 30 seconds. Plug back in and attempt to power on.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the LED driver board
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the back panel. Locate the LED driver board (usually a separate small board or a section of the power supply board with high-voltage output connectors going to the backlight strips). Check for burn marks, swollen capacitors, or loose connectors.

fixSteps.title: Replace the LED driver or backlight strips
fixSteps.instruction: If the LED driver board is faulty, replace it with a compatible unit. If LED strips are burned out, the entire LED strip set must be replaced, which requires full disassembly of the panel. This is a complex repair best left to professionals.
tools: replacement LED driver or LED strips

whenToCallPro: LED strip replacement requires full panel disassembly and carries a high risk of cracking the LCD panel. This repair is recommended for professional technicians only.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV 4 blinks red light fix backlight LED driver

faq.q: Can I still use my TV if the backlight failed?
faq.a: The TV cannot produce a visible image without a working backlight. The screen will be completely dark even though audio may still work.

faq.q: Is it worth repairing a backlight failure?
faq.a: For newer high-end models, backlight repair at $200-$450 is usually worthwhile. For older or budget models, the repair cost may approach the replacement cost of a new TV.

faq.q: How do I know if it is the driver board or the LED strips?
faq.a: A technician can test the LED driver output voltage. If voltage is present but the strips do not illuminate, the strips are faulty. If no voltage is output, the driver board is the issue.

title: Main Board or T-Con Board Failure
shortDescription: The standby LED blinks six times repeatedly, indicating a main board or T-Con board malfunction.
fullDescription:
A six-blink error code on Sony Bravia TVs indicates a failure in the main processing board or the T-Con (timing control) board. The main board handles all input processing, smart TV functions, and sends video signals to the T-Con board, which then drives the LCD panel.

This error often manifests after a firmware update failure, overheating, or gradual component degradation. The T-Con board is responsible for translating the video signal into the precise timing signals that control each pixel on the LCD panel.

In some cases, the error can be resolved by reflowing solder joints on the main board or replacing specific ICs. However, full board replacement is the most reliable fix. Before replacing hardware, always attempt a factory reset and firmware update via USB.

causes.cause: Main board processor or memory failure
T-Con board failure
Failed firmware update corrupting the main board software
Overheating damage to main board components

symptoms: Standby LED blinks red six times in a repeating pattern
TV does not power on at all
TV powers on but displays distorted or garbled image
TV freezes on the Sony logo during boot
Vertical or horizontal lines on the screen

fixSteps.title: Power reset the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV for 60 seconds. Press and hold the power button on the TV for 30 seconds to discharge. Plug back in and try to power on.

fixSteps.title: Attempt a forced firmware update via USB
fixSteps.instruction: Download the latest firmware for your specific model from Sony's support website. Extract the update file to a USB flash drive formatted as FAT32. Insert the USB into the TV and follow the on-screen or forced-update procedure described in your model's service manual.

fixSteps.title: Inspect main board and T-Con board
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the back panel. Inspect the main board and T-Con board for visible damage: swollen capacitors, burn marks, or corroded solder joints. Check all ribbon cable connections between the boards.

fixSteps.title: Replace the faulty board
fixSteps.instruction: If the main board is faulty, replace it with a compatible replacement. If the T-Con board is faulty, replace it. Ensure all ribbon cables are firmly seated after installation.
tools: replacement main board or T-Con board

whenToCallPro: If a firmware update via USB does not resolve the issue and you are not experienced with board-level TV repair, contact Sony support or a qualified TV repair technician.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV 6 blinks red light fix main board T-Con

faq.q: Can a firmware update fix the 6-blink error?
faq.a: In some cases, yes. If the error was caused by a corrupted firmware update, reflashing via USB can resolve it. However, if the hardware has failed, firmware will not help.

faq.q: What is a T-Con board?
faq.a: The T-Con (Timing Control) board converts the video signal from the main board into the precise electrical signals that control each pixel on the LCD panel. It sits between the main board and the panel itself.

faq.q: How do I tell if it is the main board or T-Con board?
faq.a: If the TV does not boot at all, the main board is more likely at fault. If the TV boots but shows distorted video, lines, or color issues, the T-Con board is the more likely culprit.

title: Panel or Logic Board Critical Failure
shortDescription: The standby LED blinks eight times repeatedly, indicating a critical panel or logic board failure.
fullDescription:
An eight-blink error on Sony Bravia TVs signals a severe hardware failure, typically involving the LCD/OLED panel itself or a critical logic board failure. This is one of the most serious blink codes and often indicates damage that may not be economically repairable.

The 8-blink code can be triggered by physical damage to the panel, a catastrophic failure in the panel driver ICs, or a complete main board failure where the processor cannot initialize. On OLED models, this may indicate a panel driver or OLED driver IC failure.

Before assuming the worst, perform a power reset and check all internal cable connections. In rare cases, a loose ribbon cable between the T-Con board and the panel can trigger this code.

causes.cause: LCD/OLED panel failure or physical damage
Panel driver IC failure
Complete main board failure
Loose or damaged ribbon cable between T-Con and panel

symptoms: Standby LED blinks red eight times in a repeating pattern
TV completely unresponsive
No image, no sound, no backlight
TV may emit a brief click then shut down immediately

fixSteps.title: Power reset the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV from the wall outlet and wait 60 seconds. Press and hold the power button on the TV for 30 seconds. Plug back in and try to power on.

fixSteps.title: Check internal ribbon cables
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the back panel. Carefully check all ribbon cables connecting the T-Con board to the LCD panel. Reseat any loose connections. Look for torn or damaged ribbon cables.

fixSteps.title: Seek professional diagnosis
fixSteps.instruction: If the power reset and cable check do not resolve the issue, the TV likely has a panel or critical board failure. Contact Sony support or a qualified repair shop for a professional diagnosis to determine if the repair is economically viable.

whenToCallPro: The 8-blink code almost always requires professional diagnosis. If the TV is under warranty, contact Sony support immediately. For out-of-warranty TVs, get a repair estimate before deciding whether to repair or replace.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV 8 blinks red light fix panel failure

faq.q: Is an 8-blink error worth repairing?
faq.a: It depends on the cause. If it is a loose cable, the fix is cheap. If the panel has failed, the repair cost often exceeds 50% of a new TV price, making replacement more economical.

faq.q: Does Sony warranty cover the 8-blink error?
faq.a: If the TV is within its standard warranty period (typically 1 year) or covered by an extended warranty, Sony should cover the repair at no cost. Contact Sony support with your model number and purchase date.

faq.q: Can the 8-blink error be caused by a power surge?
faq.a: Yes, a severe power surge can damage the panel driver ICs or main board, triggering the 8-blink code. A surge protector is recommended for all TVs.

title: No Signal on Selected Input
shortDescription: The TV displays a 'No Signal' message when an input source is selected.
fullDescription:
The 'No Signal' message appears on Sony Bravia TVs when the selected HDMI or other input port is not receiving a valid video signal from the connected device. This is one of the most common issues reported by Sony TV owners and is usually caused by a connection or configuration problem rather than a hardware failure.

The issue can stem from a loose or faulty HDMI cable, the connected device being powered off or in standby, selecting the wrong input on the TV, or an HDMI handshake failure between the TV and the source device. In some cases, HDMI port hardware failure on either the TV or the source device can also cause this error.

Most No Signal issues can be resolved by checking connections and cycling power on both devices. HDMI handshake problems are particularly common when using AV receivers, soundbars, or HDMI switches between the source and the TV.

causes.cause: Loose or disconnected HDMI cable
Wrong input source selected on the TV
Connected device is powered off or in standby mode
Faulty or low-quality HDMI cable
HDMI handshake failure
HDMI port hardware failure on the TV

symptoms: 'No Signal' message displayed on screen
Black screen with 'No Signal' overlay
Input icon shows but no picture from the connected device
TV keeps switching to 'No Signal' screen

fixSteps.title: Check the HDMI cable connection
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the HDMI cable from both the TV and the source device. Inspect the connectors for bent pins or debris. Firmly replug the cable into both devices.

fixSteps.title: Select the correct input
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Input or Source button on the Sony remote. Select the HDMI port that your device is connected to (HDMI 1, HDMI 2, etc.). Make sure you match the physical port to the on-screen selection.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle both devices
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the TV and the connected device. Unplug both from power for 30 seconds. Plug the source device in first and turn it on. Then plug in the TV and turn it on. Select the correct input.

fixSteps.title: Try a different HDMI cable and port
fixSteps.instruction: Swap the HDMI cable with a known working cable (preferably High Speed or Ultra High Speed rated). Also try a different HDMI port on the TV to rule out a port failure.

fixSteps.title: Adjust HDMI signal format
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Channels & Inputs > External inputs > HDMI signal format. Try switching between Standard and Enhanced format for the affected HDMI port. Some devices require Enhanced format for 4K HDR signals.

whenToCallPro: If the issue persists after trying all HDMI ports with multiple cables and devices, the TV's HDMI input board may have failed and requires professional repair.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV no signal HDMI fix

faq.q: Why does my Sony TV say No Signal when everything is connected?
faq.a: The most common cause is an HDMI handshake failure. Power cycling both devices usually resolves it. Also verify you have the correct input selected on the TV.

faq.q: Do I need a special HDMI cable for 4K?
faq.a: Yes, for 4K at 60Hz with HDR you need at minimum a Premium High Speed HDMI cable. For 4K at 120Hz, you need an Ultra High Speed HDMI 2.1 cable.

faq.q: Can a faulty HDMI cable cause No Signal?
faq.a: Absolutely. HDMI cables can develop internal breaks or have poor-quality connectors. Always try a different cable before assuming a hardware problem.

title: WiFi Network Connection Failure
shortDescription: The TV cannot establish or maintain a connection to the WiFi network.
fullDescription:
Sony Bravia TVs with Google TV or Android TV rely on a WiFi connection for streaming apps, software updates, and smart features. When the TV fails to connect to WiFi, it may display various error messages including 'WiFi Connection Failed,' 'Cannot Connect to Network,' or simply fail to find available networks.

This issue can be caused by incorrect WiFi password entry, router compatibility problems, signal interference, TV software bugs, or hardware failure of the TV's built-in WiFi module. Sony TVs may also have issues with certain WiFi security protocols or newer WiFi 6 router configurations.

The TV's WiFi performance can be affected by distance from the router, physical obstructions like walls and large appliances, and interference from other wireless devices. Some Sony TVs also have known issues with 5GHz networks and may work more reliably on the 2.4GHz band.

causes.cause: Incorrect WiFi password entered
Router too far away or signal interference
TV software bug affecting WiFi connectivity
Router compatibility issue with TV WiFi module
Failed WiFi module in the TV

symptoms: TV cannot find any WiFi networks
TV finds the network but fails to connect
WiFi connects but disconnects frequently
Streaming apps show 'No Internet' errors
TV software update fails due to network issues

fixSteps.title: Restart the TV and router
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV from power for 60 seconds. Also unplug your WiFi router for 30 seconds. Plug the router back in first and wait for it to fully boot (2-3 minutes). Then plug in the TV and try connecting to WiFi again.

fixSteps.title: Re-enter WiFi credentials
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network & Internet > WiFi. Forget the saved network by selecting it and choosing 'Forget.' Then reconnect by selecting your network and carefully entering the password. Use 'Show password' to verify accuracy.

fixSteps.title: Change WiFi frequency band
fixSteps.instruction: If your router broadcasts separate 2.4GHz and 5GHz networks, try connecting to the 2.4GHz network. It has better range and wall penetration. If using a combined network, configure your router to broadcast separate bands.

fixSteps.title: Set static IP and DNS
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network & Internet > WiFi > your network > IP settings. Change from DHCP to Static. Set the IP address to an unused address in your network range, use your router's IP as the gateway, and set DNS to 8.8.8.8 (primary) and 8.8.4.4 (secondary).

fixSteps.title: Factory reset the TV
fixSteps.instruction: If all else fails, go to Settings > System > About > Reset > Factory data reset. This will erase all settings and installed apps. After the reset, set up the TV again and try connecting to WiFi.

whenToCallPro: If the TV cannot find any WiFi networks at all even after a factory reset, the internal WiFi module may have failed. Contact Sony support or use a wired Ethernet connection as an alternative.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV WiFi not connecting fix

faq.q: Can I use a wired Ethernet connection instead of WiFi?
faq.a: Yes, all Sony Bravia smart TVs have an Ethernet port. A wired connection is more stable and faster than WiFi. Simply connect an Ethernet cable from your router to the TV.

faq.q: Why does my Sony TV keep disconnecting from WiFi?
faq.a: Frequent disconnections are usually caused by weak signal strength, interference from other devices, or a TV software bug. Try moving the router closer, using the 2.4GHz band, or updating the TV firmware.

faq.q: Will a factory reset fix WiFi problems?
faq.a: A factory reset can fix software-related WiFi issues. It will erase all your settings, apps, and accounts, so use it as a last resort.

title: HDMI ARC/eARC Audio Output Failure
shortDescription: No audio output through the HDMI ARC or eARC connection to a soundbar or AV receiver.
fullDescription:
HDMI ARC (Audio Return Channel) and eARC (Enhanced Audio Return Channel) allow audio to be sent from the TV to a soundbar or AV receiver through a single HDMI cable. When this connection fails, the TV may play audio through its built-in speakers or produce no sound at all, even though the picture displays normally.

ARC/eARC issues on Sony Bravia TVs are commonly caused by incorrect audio output settings, HDMI-CEC configuration problems, incompatible audio format settings, or HDMI handshake failures. The TV must be configured to output audio through the external speaker system rather than the internal speakers.

Sony TVs have a specific HDMI port designated for ARC (usually HDMI 3 or the port labeled ARC/eARC). Connecting to the wrong port will not carry audio back to the external device. Additionally, the HDMI cable must support ARC, and for eARC features like Dolby Atmos passthrough, an Ultra High Speed HDMI cable is required.

causes.cause: TV audio output not set to external speaker
HDMI-CEC (Bravia Sync) disabled or misconfigured
Connected to wrong HDMI port (not ARC-labeled)
Incompatible audio format selected
HDMI cable does not support ARC
HDMI ARC handshake failure

symptoms: No sound from soundbar or AV receiver
Audio plays from TV speakers instead of soundbar
Sound cuts in and out intermittently
Dolby Atmos or surround sound not working
Audio delay or lip sync issues through ARC

fixSteps.title: Verify the correct HDMI port
fixSteps.instruction: Check that your soundbar or AV receiver is connected to the HDMI port labeled 'ARC' or 'eARC' on the TV. This is typically HDMI 3 on most Sony Bravia models. Check the labels on the back panel of the TV.

fixSteps.title: Configure audio output settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Display & Sound > Audio output. Set 'Speakers' to 'Audio system.' Enable 'eARC mode' if available. Under 'Digital audio out,' select 'Auto 1' or 'Pass through' for surround sound content.

fixSteps.title: Enable Bravia Sync (HDMI-CEC)
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Channels & Inputs > External inputs > Bravia Sync settings. Turn on 'Bravia Sync control.' Turn on 'Device auto power off' and 'TV auto power on.' This allows the TV and soundbar to communicate.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle both devices
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug both the TV and the soundbar/receiver. Wait 30 seconds. Plug in the soundbar first and turn it on. Then plug in the TV and turn it on. The ARC connection should re-establish automatically.

fixSteps.title: Try a different HDMI cable
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the HDMI cable with a Premium High Speed or Ultra High Speed HDMI cable. Older or low-quality cables may not support ARC properly. For eARC and Dolby Atmos, an Ultra High Speed HDMI 2.1 cable is required.

whenToCallPro: If ARC does not work with any soundbar or receiver after trying all troubleshooting steps, the HDMI ARC port hardware on the TV may be faulty. Contact Sony support for repair options.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV HDMI ARC eARC no sound fix

faq.q: What is the difference between ARC and eARC?
faq.a: ARC supports compressed surround sound (Dolby Digital, DTS). eARC supports uncompressed and high-bitrate audio including Dolby Atmos and DTS:X. eARC requires an HDMI 2.1 cable.

faq.q: Which HDMI port supports ARC on Sony TVs?
faq.a: The ARC/eARC port is typically HDMI 3 on most Sony Bravia models, but check the labels on the back of your TV. Only one port supports ARC.

faq.q: Do I need a special HDMI cable for ARC?
faq.a: Standard ARC works with most HDMI cables. For eARC, you need a Premium High Speed or Ultra High Speed HDMI cable that supports the Ethernet channel.

title: Software/Firmware Update Failure
shortDescription: The TV fails to download or install a software update, displaying an update error.
fullDescription:
Sony Bravia TVs with Google TV or Android TV receive periodic software updates that include bug fixes, new features, and security patches. When an update fails, the TV may display errors during download, installation, or restart after the update.

Update failures can occur due to unstable internet connections during download, insufficient storage space on the TV, power interruptions during installation, or corrupted update files. In some cases, a previous failed update can leave the system in an inconsistent state that prevents future updates.

If an over-the-air (OTA) update fails repeatedly, Sony provides firmware files for manual installation via USB on their support website. This method bypasses potential network issues and is the most reliable way to update the TV firmware.

causes.cause: Unstable or slow internet connection during download
Insufficient storage space on the TV
Power interruption during update installation
Corrupted update file
Previous failed update leaving system in inconsistent state

symptoms: Update download progress bar stops or fails
'Software update failed' error message
TV stuck on update screen during restart
TV repeatedly prompts for update that keeps failing
TV bootloops after a failed update

fixSteps.title: Retry the update over network
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure a stable internet connection (wired Ethernet preferred). Go to Settings > System > About > System software update > Software update. Select 'Check for update' and allow it to download and install.

fixSteps.title: Clear storage space
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Apps > See all apps. Uninstall unused apps or clear cache for large apps. The TV needs sufficient free space to download and install updates.

fixSteps.title: Update via USB
fixSteps.instruction: Visit Sony's support website (sony.com/electronics/support). Search for your TV model. Download the latest firmware update file. Copy the file to a FAT32-formatted USB flash drive. Insert the USB into the TV and go to Settings > System > About > System software update > USB update.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset and retry
fixSteps.instruction: If updates consistently fail, go to Settings > System > About > Reset > Factory data reset. After the reset, set up the TV again and attempt the update. This clears any corrupted system files that may be blocking the update.

whenToCallPro: If the TV is stuck in a boot loop after a failed update and cannot be recovered via USB update or factory reset, contact Sony support. The TV may need a service-level firmware flash.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV software update failed fix USB firmware

faq.q: Can I skip a software update on my Sony TV?
faq.a: You can postpone updates, but they cannot be permanently skipped on Google TV. For security and compatibility, keeping the TV updated is recommended.

faq.q: Will a factory reset delete my apps?
faq.a: Yes, a factory reset erases all installed apps, saved accounts, and customized settings. You will need to set up the TV from scratch and reinstall apps.

faq.q: What happens if the power goes out during an update?
faq.a: A power interruption during a firmware update can corrupt the system software, potentially causing boot loops. If this happens, try the USB update method to recover the TV.

title: Netflix App Error or Crash
shortDescription: The Netflix app crashes, fails to load, or displays error codes on the Sony Bravia TV.
fullDescription:
Netflix errors on Sony Bravia TVs can manifest as app crashes, loading failures, specific Netflix error codes (such as NW-2-5, UI-800-3, or TVQ-ST-131), or the app failing to launch at all. These issues are common on both Android TV and Google TV platforms.

Netflix errors are frequently caused by corrupted app data or cache, outdated Netflix app versions, network connectivity problems, or incompatibility between the Netflix app version and the TV's operating system version. In some cases, Sony TV firmware updates can temporarily break Netflix compatibility until a Netflix app update is released.

Most Netflix issues can be resolved by clearing the app cache and data, updating the app, or reinstalling it. Persistent issues may require a TV firmware update or factory reset.

causes.cause: Corrupted Netflix app cache or data
Outdated Netflix app version
Network connectivity issues
TV firmware incompatibility with Netflix app
Insufficient TV storage for app operation

symptoms: Netflix app crashes immediately after opening
Netflix displays error code (NW-2-5, UI-800-3, TVQ-ST-131)
Netflix stuck on loading screen
Netflix video stutters or fails to play
Netflix app disappears from the home screen

fixSteps.title: Clear Netflix app cache and data
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Apps > See all apps > Netflix. Select 'Clear cache' then 'Clear data.' This will sign you out of Netflix. Reopen the app and sign in again.

fixSteps.title: Update the Netflix app
fixSteps.instruction: Open the Google Play Store on the TV. Search for Netflix. If an update is available, select 'Update.' Restart the TV after updating.

fixSteps.title: Uninstall and reinstall Netflix
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Apps > See all apps > Netflix. Select 'Uninstall updates' (Netflix cannot be fully uninstalled as it is a system app). Then open the Google Play Store and install the latest version.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV from the wall outlet for 60 seconds. This clears the temporary memory and can resolve stubborn app issues. Plug back in and try Netflix again.

fixSteps.title: Check for TV firmware update
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > System > About > System software update. Install any available updates. Netflix compatibility fixes are often included in firmware updates.

whenToCallPro: Netflix app issues are almost always software-related and rarely require professional help. If the issue persists after all steps, contact Sony support to check for known compatibility issues with your specific TV model.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV Netflix not working error fix

faq.q: Will clearing Netflix data delete my account?
faq.a: No, clearing data only removes the local cache and signs you out. Your Netflix account, watch history, and preferences are stored on Netflix's servers and will be restored when you sign in again.

faq.q: What does Netflix error NW-2-5 mean?
faq.a: NW-2-5 indicates a network connectivity issue preventing the TV from reaching Netflix servers. Check your internet connection, restart your router, and try again.

faq.q: Why does Netflix keep crashing on my Sony TV?
faq.a: Frequent crashes are usually caused by corrupted app data or an outdated app version. Clear the app data and update to the latest version from the Google Play Store.

title: YouTube App Error or Playback Failure
shortDescription: The YouTube app crashes, fails to load content, or shows playback errors on the Sony Bravia TV.
fullDescription:
YouTube app errors on Sony Bravia TVs can range from app crashes and loading failures to video playback issues and buffering problems. Since YouTube is one of the most commonly used apps on smart TVs, these issues affect a large number of users.

The most common causes include corrupted app data, outdated app versions, network bandwidth limitations, and Google account synchronization problems. YouTube requires a stable internet connection with sufficient bandwidth for streaming — at least 3 Mbps for SD, 7 Mbps for HD, and 20 Mbps for 4K content.

On Google TV models, YouTube integration issues can also be caused by conflicts with the Google TV home screen launcher, which heavily features YouTube content. Clearing the Google TV home app cache alongside the YouTube app cache can resolve these issues.

causes.cause: Corrupted YouTube app cache or data
Outdated YouTube app version
Insufficient network bandwidth for streaming
Google account sync issue
TV operating system bug

symptoms: YouTube app crashes on launch
Videos fail to play or buffer endlessly
'An error occurred' message in YouTube
YouTube search not returning results
YouTube recommendations not loading on Google TV home screen

fixSteps.title: Clear YouTube app cache and data
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Apps > See all apps > YouTube. Select 'Clear cache' then 'Clear data.' Reopen YouTube and sign in again.

fixSteps.title: Update the YouTube app
fixSteps.instruction: Open the Google Play Store on the TV. Search for YouTube. Install any available updates. Restart the TV after updating.

fixSteps.title: Check network speed
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network & Internet > Network status. Run a connection test. Ensure download speeds are at least 7 Mbps for HD or 20 Mbps for 4K YouTube content. If speeds are low, restart your router or move it closer to the TV.

fixSteps.title: Re-sign into Google account
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Accounts & sign-in. Remove your Google account. Restart the TV. Then add the Google account back. This refreshes the authentication tokens used by YouTube.

whenToCallPro: YouTube app issues are software-related and do not require professional repair. If all troubleshooting steps fail, a factory reset is the last resort before contacting Sony support.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV YouTube not working fix

faq.q: Why does YouTube buffer on my Sony TV but works fine on my phone?
faq.a: The TV may have a weaker WiFi connection than your phone. TVs typically have smaller antennas. Try using a wired Ethernet connection or moving the router closer to the TV.

faq.q: Can I use YouTube without a Google account on Sony TV?
faq.a: Yes, you can use YouTube without signing in, but you will not have access to subscriptions, watch history, or personalized recommendations.

faq.q: Will clearing YouTube data lose my subscriptions?
faq.a: No, your subscriptions and watch history are stored in your Google account in the cloud. They will be restored when you sign back in.

title: Chromecast Built-in Not Working
shortDescription: The built-in Chromecast feature fails to discover or receive cast sessions from mobile devices.
fullDescription:
Sony Bravia TVs with Android TV or Google TV include Chromecast Built-in, allowing users to cast content from smartphones, tablets, and computers directly to the TV. When Chromecast fails, the TV may not appear as a cast target, cast sessions may fail to connect, or casting may disconnect during playback.

Chromecast Built-in requires both the TV and the casting device to be on the same WiFi network. Network isolation features, guest networks, and some router configurations can prevent Chromecast from working. The feature also relies on multicast/mDNS traffic being allowed on the network.

Common issues include the TV not appearing in the cast device list, cast sessions timing out during connection, video casting working but audio not transmitting, or the cast session disconnecting after a few seconds. These issues are usually resolved by ensuring both devices are on the same network and restarting the TV.

causes.cause: TV and casting device on different WiFi networks
Router blocking multicast/mDNS traffic
TV Chromecast service crashed or not running
Outdated Google Play Services on the TV
Network firewall or AP isolation blocking discovery

symptoms: TV does not appear in cast device list on phone
Cast button grayed out in apps
Cast session starts but disconnects immediately
Video casts but no audio
'Could not connect to device' error when casting

fixSteps.title: Verify same WiFi network
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure both the TV and the casting device are connected to the exact same WiFi network name. Check the TV's network at Settings > Network & Internet. Check your phone's WiFi settings to confirm they match.

fixSteps.title: Restart the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV from power for 60 seconds. This restarts the Chromecast Built-in service. Plug back in and wait for the TV to fully boot before attempting to cast.

fixSteps.title: Enable Chromecast Built-in
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Apps > See all apps > Show system apps. Find 'Chromecast built-in' and ensure it is enabled and not disabled. If it appears disabled, enable it.

fixSteps.title: Update Google Play Services
fixSteps.instruction: Open the Google Play Store on the TV. Search for 'Google Play Services.' Install any available updates. These updates include Chromecast functionality improvements.

fixSteps.title: Check router settings
fixSteps.instruction: Log into your router admin panel. Ensure AP Isolation (also called Client Isolation or WiFi Isolation) is disabled. Ensure multicast/mDNS traffic is allowed. These features are sometimes enabled by default on guest networks.

whenToCallPro: Chromecast issues are almost always network or software related. If the Chromecast Built-in app is missing from your TV entirely, a factory reset should restore it. Contact Sony support only if the issue persists after a factory reset.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV Chromecast built-in not working fix

faq.q: Do I need a separate Chromecast dongle for my Sony TV?
faq.a: No, Sony Bravia TVs with Android TV or Google TV have Chromecast Built-in. No separate hardware is needed.

faq.q: Why can my phone not find the TV for casting?
faq.a: Both devices must be on the same WiFi network. Also check that AP Isolation is disabled on your router and that the Chromecast Built-in service is enabled on the TV.

faq.q: Can I cast to my Sony TV without WiFi?
faq.a: No, Chromecast requires both devices to be on the same WiFi network. However, some apps support screen mirroring via WiFi Direct, which does not require a router.

title: Audio and Video Out of Sync (Lip Sync Issue)
shortDescription: Audio is noticeably ahead of or behind the video, causing lip sync mismatch.
fullDescription:
Audio-video synchronization issues on Sony Bravia TVs manifest as the sound being noticeably ahead of or behind the on-screen action. This is particularly noticeable during dialogue scenes where lip movements do not match the spoken words.

The most common cause is audio processing delay introduced by external audio devices like soundbars and AV receivers connected via HDMI ARC, optical, or Bluetooth. Each stage of audio processing adds latency, and the TV's video processing may not match this delay. Bluetooth audio connections typically add the most latency (100-300ms).

Sony Bravia TVs include an audio-video sync adjustment feature that allows manual offset of the audio timing. This can compensate for fixed delays introduced by external audio equipment. Some TV processing modes like motion smoothing can also introduce video delay that creates sync issues.

causes.cause: Audio delay from external soundbar or AV receiver
Bluetooth audio connection latency
TV video processing modes adding delay
Streaming content encoded with audio sync offset
HDMI ARC handshake causing intermittent sync drift

symptoms: Voices do not match lip movements on screen
Sound effects are delayed relative to on-screen action
Audio arrives before the video
Sync issue only with external speakers, not TV speakers
Sync issue only on certain apps or inputs

fixSteps.title: Adjust the A/V sync setting
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Display & Sound > Audio output > A/V sync. Adjust the slider to compensate for the delay. If audio is behind video, increase the value. Test with dialogue content and adjust until lips and audio match.

fixSteps.title: Switch to wired audio connection
fixSteps.instruction: If using Bluetooth speakers or headphones, switch to HDMI ARC or optical connection. Bluetooth introduces significant latency (100-300ms) that is difficult to fully compensate for.

fixSteps.title: Disable video processing modes
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Display & Sound > Picture > Motion. Disable Motionflow or set it to 'Off.' Disable CineMotion as well. These processing features can add video delay that causes sync issues.

fixSteps.title: Use Game mode or Graphics mode
fixSteps.instruction: If the issue persists, try switching to Game mode or Graphics mode under Settings > Display & Sound > Picture > Picture mode. These modes minimize video processing delay.

fixSteps.title: Enable eARC for lossless audio passthrough
fixSteps.instruction: If using HDMI ARC, enable eARC mode at Settings > Display & Sound > Audio output > eARC mode. Set Digital audio output to 'Pass through.' eARC reduces processing overhead and can improve sync.

partsNeeded.part: HDMI ARC cable (if switching from Bluetooth)

whenToCallPro: Audio sync issues are settings-related and do not require professional repair. If the issue cannot be resolved with the A/V sync adjustment and all processing modes are disabled, there may be a firmware issue — check for TV software updates.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV audio video out of sync lip sync fix

faq.q: Why is Bluetooth audio always delayed on my TV?
faq.a: Bluetooth inherently adds latency of 100-300ms due to audio encoding and transmission. This delay is too large for perfect lip sync. Use HDMI ARC or optical for the best sync.

faq.q: Does Game mode affect picture quality?
faq.a: Game mode disables advanced video processing, which may slightly reduce picture quality but significantly reduces input lag and helps with audio-video sync.

faq.q: Can I adjust audio sync per input?
faq.a: The A/V sync setting on Sony TVs typically applies globally. You may need to readjust it when switching between different inputs or audio output methods.

title: Google TV Interface Crash or Freeze
shortDescription: The Google TV interface crashes, freezes, or becomes unresponsive on the Sony Bravia TV.
fullDescription:
Sony Bravia TVs running Google TV may experience system-level crashes where the home screen becomes unresponsive, apps fail to open, or the TV displays a 'System UI Not Responding' error. These crashes can cause the TV to become completely frozen, requiring a forced restart.

Google TV crashes are typically caused by insufficient system resources, excessive cached data from multiple apps, corrupted system cache, or software bugs in specific Google TV versions. The Google TV interface is resource-intensive, and on models with limited RAM, running multiple apps in the background can exhaust available memory.

Periodic restarts of the TV can help prevent Google TV crashes by clearing temporary memory. Sony also recommends keeping the TV software updated to the latest version, as many stability fixes are included in firmware updates.

causes.cause: Insufficient system memory (RAM exhaustion)
Excessive cached data from installed apps
Corrupted system cache partition
Google TV software bug in current firmware
Incompatible or buggy sideloaded app

symptoms: Home screen freezes and does not respond to remote
'System UI Not Responding' dialog appears
Apps take extremely long to open or fail to launch
TV reboots itself unexpectedly
Remote control inputs are severely delayed

fixSteps.title: Force restart the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the power button on the remote for about 5 seconds until the TV restarts. Alternatively, unplug the TV from the wall for 60 seconds and plug it back in.

fixSteps.title: Clear system cache
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > System > About > Restart. After the TV restarts, go to Settings > Apps > See all apps. For each large app, select it and clear the cache. Pay particular attention to Google TV Home, Google Play Services, and YouTube.

fixSteps.title: Uninstall unnecessary apps
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Apps > See all apps. Uninstall apps you do not use. Each installed app consumes storage and may run background processes that consume RAM.

fixSteps.title: Disable home screen recommendations
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Accounts & sign-in > your Google account > toggle off personalized recommendations. This reduces the processing load on the Google TV home screen.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset as last resort
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > System > About > Reset > Factory data reset. This clears all data and restores the TV to its original state. Set up the TV again and install only the apps you need.

whenToCallPro: Google TV crashes are software-related and do not require professional hardware repair. If the TV crashes repeatedly even after a factory reset and firmware update, contact Sony support to check for known issues with your model.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia Google TV crashing freezing fix

faq.q: How often should I restart my Sony TV?
faq.a: Restarting the TV once a week helps clear temporary files and free up memory. This can prevent many Google TV performance issues.

faq.q: Can I switch from Google TV to a simpler interface?
faq.a: You cannot fully replace Google TV on Sony Bravia TVs. However, you can use a streaming device like Apple TV or Roku and switch the TV input to bypass Google TV.

faq.q: Does the number of installed apps affect TV performance?
faq.a: Yes, each app consumes storage and many run background processes. Keeping only the apps you actively use helps maintain system performance.

title: HDMI Handshake / HDCP Authentication Failure
shortDescription: The TV displays a black screen, purple screen, or flashing image due to an HDMI handshake or HDCP authentication failure.
fullDescription:
HDMI handshake errors occur when the TV and a connected device fail to complete the HDCP (High-bandwidth Digital Content Protection) authentication process. HDCP is a copy protection technology that requires the source device and the display to verify each other before transmitting protected content.

When the handshake fails, the TV may display a completely black screen, a purple or green screen, a flashing or flickering image, or a message indicating that the content is protected. This is particularly common when using HDMI splitters, AV receivers, or long HDMI cable runs that can degrade the HDCP signal.

Sony Bravia TVs support HDCP 2.2 and HDCP 2.3 for 4K content. If any device in the HDMI chain does not support the required HDCP version, the handshake will fail. Streaming devices, Blu-ray players, and game consoles all use HDCP and can trigger this issue.

causes.cause: HDMI cable not supporting HDCP
HDMI splitter or switch breaking the HDCP chain
Power-on sequence causing handshake timing issue
AV receiver not passing HDCP authentication
Firmware bug in connected device

symptoms: Black screen when HDMI device is connected
Purple or green screen on HDMI input
Flickering or flashing image
'HDCP not supported' or similar error message
Audio works but no video

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the HDMI chain
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the TV, source device, and any AV receiver or switch in between. Unplug all devices from power for 30 seconds. Plug in and turn on the TV first. Then turn on the AV receiver. Finally, turn on the source device. This establishes a clean handshake sequence.

fixSteps.title: Reseat the HDMI cable
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the HDMI cable from both ends. Inspect for bent pins or damage. Firmly plug it back in. Try a different HDMI cable, preferably a Premium High Speed or Ultra High Speed certified cable.

fixSteps.title: Remove HDMI splitters or switches
fixSteps.instruction: If using an HDMI splitter, switch, or adapter, temporarily remove it and connect the source device directly to the TV. Many HDMI splitters do not properly pass HDCP authentication.

fixSteps.title: Change HDMI signal format on the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Channels & Inputs > External inputs > HDMI signal format. Toggle the affected HDMI port between Standard and Enhanced. Standard uses HDCP 1.4, Enhanced uses HDCP 2.2/2.3 for 4K content.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware on all devices
fixSteps.instruction: Check for and install firmware updates on the TV, source device, and AV receiver. HDCP compatibility fixes are frequently included in firmware updates.

whenToCallPro: HDMI handshake issues are typically resolved through cable replacement and power cycling. If the issue persists with direct connections and new cables, the HDMI port on the TV may have a hardware fault requiring professional repair.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV HDMI HDCP handshake error black screen fix

faq.q: What is HDCP and why does it cause problems?
faq.a: HDCP (High-bandwidth Digital Content Protection) is copy protection built into HDMI. All devices in the chain must support the same HDCP version. If any device fails authentication, the signal is blocked.

faq.q: Does the power-on order really matter?
faq.a: Yes, HDCP handshake timing is sensitive. Turning on the TV first, then the receiver, then the source device gives the best results for establishing a clean authentication.

faq.q: Can an old HDMI cable cause handshake failures?
faq.a: Yes, older cables may not reliably carry HDCP 2.2/2.3 signals. Always use Premium High Speed or Ultra High Speed certified HDMI cables.

title: Bluetooth Device Pairing Failure
shortDescription: The TV fails to pair with Bluetooth headphones, speakers, or other Bluetooth devices.
fullDescription:
Sony Bravia TVs support Bluetooth audio output for headphones and speakers, as well as Bluetooth input devices like keyboards and game controllers. When Bluetooth pairing fails, the TV may not discover the device, fail during the pairing process, or lose the connection immediately after pairing.

Bluetooth pairing issues are commonly caused by the Bluetooth device not being in pairing mode, the TV's Bluetooth being disabled, too many paired devices, interference from other wireless devices, or incompatible Bluetooth profiles. Sony TVs typically support A2DP for audio and HID for input devices.

Some Bluetooth audio codecs are not supported by the TV, which can cause pairing to succeed but audio to not work. Sony Bravia TVs generally support SBC and AAC Bluetooth codecs. Higher quality codecs like aptX or LDAC may not be available on all models.

causes.cause: Bluetooth device not in pairing mode
TV Bluetooth disabled
Too many paired devices on the TV
Incompatible Bluetooth profile or codec
Interference from other wireless devices

symptoms: TV does not find the Bluetooth device during scan
Pairing process starts but fails to complete
Paired device disconnects immediately
No audio through paired Bluetooth headphones
Bluetooth device shown as paired but not connected

fixSteps.title: Put the Bluetooth device in pairing mode
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the Bluetooth headphones or speaker is in pairing mode (typically by holding the power or Bluetooth button until the LED flashes rapidly). Refer to the device's manual for the exact pairing procedure.

fixSteps.title: Enable Bluetooth on the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Remotes & accessories > Bluetooth settings. Ensure Bluetooth is turned on. Select 'Pair new device' to scan for available devices.

fixSteps.title: Remove old paired devices
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Remotes & accessories. Review the list of paired devices. Remove devices you no longer use by selecting them and choosing 'Forget.' The TV has a limit on paired devices.

fixSteps.title: Restart both devices
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the Bluetooth device completely. Unplug the TV from power for 60 seconds. Plug the TV back in and wait for it to boot. Turn on the Bluetooth device in pairing mode and try again.

fixSteps.title: Reset the Bluetooth device
fixSteps.instruction: Many Bluetooth headphones and speakers have a reset procedure that clears all previous pairings. Refer to the device manual. After resetting, put it in pairing mode and try connecting to the TV again.

whenToCallPro: Bluetooth pairing issues are almost always resolved by the user. If the TV cannot find any Bluetooth devices after a factory reset, the Bluetooth module may be defective. Contact Sony support for diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV Bluetooth pairing not working fix

faq.q: How many Bluetooth devices can I pair with my Sony TV?
faq.a: Most Sony Bravia TVs can have up to 5-10 paired Bluetooth devices, but only one audio device can be active at a time.

faq.q: Will Bluetooth audio have a delay?
faq.a: Yes, Bluetooth audio typically has 100-300ms of latency, which can cause noticeable lip sync issues. HDMI ARC or optical connections are recommended for watching video content.

faq.q: Can I use Bluetooth headphones and TV speakers at the same time?
faq.a: This depends on the TV model. Some Sony Bravia models support simultaneous audio output to both Bluetooth and TV speakers. Check Settings > Display & Sound > Audio output for this option.

title: TV Overheating and Thermal Shutdown
shortDescription: The TV shuts down or displays a warning due to internal overheating.
fullDescription:
Sony Bravia TVs have internal temperature sensors that monitor the operating temperature of critical components. When the internal temperature exceeds safe limits, the TV will display a warning message, reduce brightness, or shut down automatically to prevent permanent damage to the panel and electronics.

Overheating is commonly caused by restricted airflow around the TV, placing the TV in an enclosed cabinet or alcove, direct sunlight exposure, high ambient room temperatures, or dust accumulation on the internal ventilation openings. TVs mounted flush against a wall with minimal gap can also overheat.

OLED models like the Sony A95K and A80 series are particularly susceptible to overheating as OLED panels generate more heat than LED panels. These models require adequate ventilation around the back panel to dissipate heat effectively.

causes.cause: Restricted airflow around the TV (enclosed cabinet)
Dust accumulation blocking ventilation openings
Direct sunlight heating the TV
High ambient room temperature
Internal cooling fan failure (models with fans)

symptoms: TV displays overheating warning message
TV shuts down unexpectedly during use
Screen brightness automatically dims
TV back panel is very hot to the touch
TV powers on but shuts off after 10-30 minutes

fixSteps.title: Improve airflow around the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure at least 4 inches (10 cm) of clearance on all sides and above the TV. If the TV is in a cabinet or alcove, open the doors or move the TV to an open location. Remove any objects placed on top of or directly behind the TV.

fixSteps.title: Clean ventilation openings
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug the TV. Use compressed air to blow dust out of the ventilation slots on the back and sides of the TV. Do not use a vacuum cleaner directly on the openings as static can damage electronics.

fixSteps.title: Reduce heat sources
fixSteps.instruction: Move the TV away from direct sunlight, radiators, fireplaces, or other heat sources. Close blinds or curtains if sunlight hits the TV. Lower the room temperature if possible.

fixSteps.title: Reduce brightness and processing
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Display & Sound > Picture > Brightness. Reduce the brightness level. Disable 'Peak luminance' or HDR tone mapping if the TV is in a warm environment. Lower brightness reduces heat generation.

fixSteps.title: Let the TV cool down
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the TV and unplug it. Wait at least 30 minutes for internal components to cool. Plug it back in and see if the overheating issue recurs. If it does with adequate ventilation, an internal cooling component may have failed.

whenToCallPro: If the TV overheats with adequate ventilation and a clean environment, an internal cooling fan or thermal management component may have failed. Contact Sony support for professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV overheating shutting down fix

faq.q: Is it normal for the back of my Sony TV to feel warm?
faq.a: Yes, it is normal for the back panel to feel warm during operation, especially on OLED models. However, if it feels uncomfortably hot to the touch, ventilation may be insufficient.

faq.q: Can overheating permanently damage my TV?
faq.a: Repeated overheating can shorten the lifespan of electronic components and, on OLED panels, can accelerate organic material degradation. The thermal shutdown feature prevents immediate damage.

faq.q: Do OLED TVs run hotter than LED TVs?
faq.a: Yes, OLED panels generate more heat than LED-backlit LCD panels. OLED TVs require more ventilation space around the back panel for proper heat dissipation.

title: Remote Control Not Responding
shortDescription: The TV does not respond to the remote control via IR or Bluetooth.
fullDescription:
Sony Bravia TVs use remote controls that communicate via infrared (IR) or Bluetooth, with most modern models using Bluetooth for voice commands and basic controls, and IR as a backup. When the remote stops working, the TV becomes difficult to operate.

The most common causes are dead batteries, Bluetooth pairing loss, IR sensor obstruction, or a malfunctioned remote. Sony Bravia remotes with Google Assistant use Bluetooth for voice and some button presses, meaning the remote may partially work (IR buttons like power and volume) but not fully work (Bluetooth functions like voice and navigation).

Before purchasing a replacement remote, try resetting the remote's Bluetooth connection and replacing the batteries. Many Sony remotes use two AA batteries. The IR sensor on the TV is typically located at the bottom center of the front bezel.

causes.cause: Dead or low batteries in the remote
Bluetooth pairing lost between remote and TV
IR sensor on TV blocked by objects or soundbar
Remote hardware failure
IR interference from other devices or bright sunlight

symptoms: TV does not respond to any remote button presses
Only some buttons work (IR works but Bluetooth does not)
Remote voice commands not working
Remote works only very close to the TV
Remote LED does not light up when pressing buttons

fixSteps.title: Replace the batteries
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the battery cover from the back of the remote. Replace both AA batteries with new ones, ensuring correct polarity (+/-). Test the remote by pressing the power button.

fixSteps.title: Re-pair the Bluetooth remote
fixSteps.instruction: On the TV, go to Settings > Remotes & accessories > Remote control > Connect via Bluetooth. On the remote, press and hold the Back and Volume Down buttons simultaneously for 3 seconds. The remote should enter pairing mode and reconnect.

fixSteps.title: Check IR line of sight
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure there are no objects blocking the path between the remote and the TV's IR sensor (located at the bottom center front of the TV). Remove any soundbar or device placed directly in front of the sensor.

fixSteps.title: Reset the remote
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the batteries from the remote. Press and hold the power button on the remote for 3 seconds to discharge it. Reinsert the batteries and try using the remote.

fixSteps.title: Use the TV buttons or Sony app
fixSteps.instruction: As a temporary workaround, use the physical buttons on the TV (typically located on the back or bottom edge). Alternatively, download the 'Google TV' app on your smartphone and use it as a virtual remote.

whenToCallPro: Remote control issues do not require professional TV repair. If the remote itself is defective, purchase a replacement Sony remote compatible with your TV model from Sony or authorized retailers.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV remote not working fix pair Bluetooth

faq.q: Can I use a universal remote with my Sony TV?
faq.a: Yes, most universal remotes support Sony TVs via IR. However, Bluetooth features like voice control will not work with universal remotes.

faq.q: How do I know if the remote IR is working?
faq.a: Point the remote at your smartphone camera and press a button. If the IR LED is working, you will see a purple or white flash on the camera screen (not visible to the naked eye).

faq.q: Can I control my Sony TV with my phone?
faq.a: Yes, download the Google TV app (formerly Android TV Remote Control) on your smartphone. Both devices must be on the same WiFi network.

title: Screen Flickering or Flashing During Use
shortDescription: The TV screen flickers, flashes, or rapidly changes brightness during viewing.
fullDescription:
Screen flickering on Sony Bravia TVs can appear as rapid brightness changes, intermittent flashing, or visible strobing of the backlight. The flickering may be constant or occur only during certain content, input sources, or brightness levels.

Flickering can be caused by TV settings such as local dimming, motion smoothing (Motionflow), ambient light sensor adjustments, or energy-saving features that dynamically adjust brightness. On LED-backlit models, backlight PWM (Pulse Width Modulation) dimming at lower brightness levels can cause visible flicker, especially in peripheral vision.

Hardware-related flickering can indicate a failing backlight driver, loose internal cable connections, or a degrading T-Con board. If the flickering is accompanied by horizontal or vertical lines, color distortion, or occurs on all inputs including the TV's own menus, it is more likely a hardware issue.

causes.cause: Local dimming or dynamic backlight feature causing flicker
Ambient light sensor rapidly adjusting brightness
Motionflow or motion smoothing causing artifacts
Energy saving mode dynamically dimming backlight
Failing backlight driver or loose cable

symptoms: Screen brightness rapidly fluctuates
Visible flashing or strobing of the backlight
Flickering in dark scenes or at low brightness
Screen flickers only on certain HDMI inputs
Flickering occurs during HDR content

fixSteps.title: Disable ambient light sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Display & Sound > Picture > Brightness > Light sensor. Turn it off. This prevents the TV from automatically adjusting brightness based on room lighting, which can cause perceived flickering.

fixSteps.title: Adjust local dimming setting
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Display & Sound > Picture > Brightness > Local dimming. Set to 'Low' or 'Off.' Aggressive local dimming can cause visible brightness fluctuations in scenes with mixed dark and bright elements.

fixSteps.title: Disable Motionflow
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Display & Sound > Picture > Motion > Motionflow. Set to 'Off.' Motion interpolation processing can cause visual artifacts that appear as flickering.

fixSteps.title: Turn off energy saving features
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > System > Power & energy > Energy saving. Set to 'Off.' Also check 'Eco' mode under picture settings and disable it. These features dim the backlight dynamically.

fixSteps.title: Test with different input sources
fixSteps.instruction: Try the built-in TV apps, different HDMI devices, and the TV menu itself. If flickering occurs on all sources including the menu, it is likely a hardware issue. If it only occurs on one input, the issue is with that source or cable.

whenToCallPro: If flickering persists with all picture processing features disabled and occurs on all inputs including the TV menu, it is likely a hardware failure in the backlight driver or T-Con board. Contact Sony support or a qualified TV repair technician.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV screen flickering flashing fix

faq.q: Is some flickering normal on LED TVs?
faq.a: Very minor flickering can be caused by PWM dimming at low brightness levels. If the flicker is noticeable during normal viewing, it should be investigated.

faq.q: Does HDR content cause flickering?
faq.a: HDR content has wider brightness range that can trigger aggressive local dimming adjustments, appearing as flicker. Reducing local dimming intensity usually helps.

faq.q: Can a firmware update fix flickering?
faq.a: If the flickering is caused by a software bug in the backlight control algorithm, a firmware update may fix it. Sony has released such fixes in the past for specific models.

title: No Audio Output from TV Speakers or External Audio
shortDescription: The TV produces no sound through internal speakers or connected external audio devices.
fullDescription:
Complete loss of audio on a Sony Bravia TV can affect the internal speakers, external audio output (HDMI ARC, optical, headphone jack), or both. The picture displays normally but no sound is produced from any source.

The most common cause is simply that the TV is muted or the volume is set to zero, or that the audio output is configured to an external device that is turned off or disconnected. Sony TVs can be set to output audio to internal speakers, external audio system, or headphones — and selecting the wrong output will result in silence from the expected output.

Less common causes include a failed speaker amplifier on the main board, damaged internal speakers, or a software glitch that has muted the audio processing pipeline. A power reset often resolves software-related audio loss.

causes.cause: TV muted or volume at zero
Audio output set to external device that is off
HDMI input audio format incompatibility
Software glitch muting audio pipeline
Internal speaker amplifier failure

symptoms: No sound from TV speakers
No sound from external soundbar or receiver
Volume indicator shows level changing but no audio output
Sound works on some inputs but not others
Sound works in TV menus but not during content playback

fixSteps.title: Check mute and volume
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Mute button on the remote to ensure the TV is not muted. Then increase the volume using the Volume Up button. Check if a volume indicator appears on screen.

fixSteps.title: Check audio output setting
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Display & Sound > Audio output > Speakers. Set to 'TV speakers' to use the built-in speakers. If using an external device, ensure it is powered on and set to the correct input.

fixSteps.title: Power reset the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV from the wall outlet for 60 seconds. Press and hold the power button on the TV for 30 seconds. Plug back in and test the audio.

fixSteps.title: Change digital audio output format
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Display & Sound > Audio output > Digital audio out. Try changing between 'PCM,' 'Auto 1,' and 'Auto 2.' Some content audio formats may not be compatible with the current setting.

fixSteps.title: Test with different sources
fixSteps.instruction: Test audio using the built-in TV apps, different HDMI inputs, and the TV tuner if available. This helps determine whether the issue is with the TV or a specific source device.

whenToCallPro: If no audio is produced through any output method (speakers, HDMI ARC, optical, headphones) after a factory reset, the audio amplifier on the main board may have failed. This requires professional board-level repair.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV no sound audio fix

faq.q: Why does my Sony TV have picture but no sound?
faq.a: The most common cause is the TV being muted, volume set to zero, or audio output directed to an external device that is turned off. Check the mute button and audio output settings first.

faq.q: Can a software update cause audio loss?
faq.a: Occasionally, a firmware update can reset audio settings or introduce a bug. A power reset after updating often resolves this. Check that the audio output has not been changed to an external device.

faq.q: Why does sound work on some channels but not others?
faq.a: Different channels may broadcast in different audio formats. If the TV is set to pass through a specific format that one channel does not support, there will be no audio. Set digital audio output to 'PCM' as a test.

title: TV Stuck on Sony Logo / Boot Loop
shortDescription: The TV gets stuck on the Sony or Google TV logo during startup and never reaches the home screen.
fullDescription:
When a Sony Bravia TV gets stuck on the startup logo — either the Sony logo or the Google TV/Android TV animation — and either stays frozen or continuously restarts (boot loop), it indicates a critical software or firmware issue that prevents the operating system from fully loading.

This problem is most commonly triggered by a failed firmware update that left the system in an inconsistent state, corrupted system files from an unexpected power loss during operation, or a malfunctioning app that crashes the system during startup. In rare cases, hardware failures like a failing eMMC storage chip can also cause boot loops.

Sony provides a recovery mechanism through forced firmware installation via USB that can restore the TV's operating system even when it cannot boot normally. This is the primary recovery method for boot loop situations.

causes.cause: Failed firmware update corrupting the operating system
Corrupted system files from power loss during operation
Problematic app crashing during system startup
Failing eMMC storage chip

symptoms: TV freezes on the Sony logo indefinitely
TV shows Google TV/Android TV animation but never progresses
TV continuously restarts (boot loop) every 30-60 seconds
TV shows 'Android is starting' followed by a restart
TV occasionally boots successfully but crashes shortly after

fixSteps.title: Perform a power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV from the wall outlet for a full 2 minutes (longer than usual). Press and hold the power button on the TV for 30 seconds while unplugged. Plug back in and attempt to power on.

fixSteps.title: Boot into Safe Mode
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the TV. Turn it on and immediately press and hold the Volume Down button on the TV (not the remote). Continue holding until the TV boots with 'Safe Mode' displayed in the corner. In Safe Mode, uninstall recently installed apps that may be causing the crash.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset via recovery mode
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the TV. Press and hold the Volume Down and Power buttons simultaneously on the TV for about 10-15 seconds. When the recovery menu appears, use the Volume buttons to navigate to 'Factory data reset' and the Power button to select. This erases all data and restores factory software.

fixSteps.title: Force firmware update via USB
fixSteps.instruction: Download the latest firmware from Sony's support site for your exact model. Copy the update file to a FAT32 USB drive. With the TV off, insert the USB. Turn the TV on — it should detect the firmware and begin installation. If not, try the recovery mode method to initiate the USB update.

whenToCallPro: If the TV does not respond to any recovery method including USB firmware flash and factory reset from recovery mode, the eMMC storage chip may have failed. This requires professional board-level repair or main board replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV stuck on logo boot loop fix recovery mode

faq.q: Will a factory reset from recovery mode delete my apps?
faq.a: Yes, a factory reset erases everything — apps, accounts, settings, and any stored data. The TV will be restored to its out-of-box state.

faq.q: How do I enter recovery mode on a Sony TV?
faq.a: Turn off the TV. Press and hold Volume Down + Power simultaneously for 10-15 seconds. The exact button combination may vary by model — check Sony's support site for your specific model.

faq.q: Can a boot loop damage the TV?
faq.a: A boot loop itself does not cause hardware damage. However, if it is caused by a failing storage chip, continued power cycling may worsen the underlying issue. Repair promptly.

title: Stuck or Dead Pixel on Screen
shortDescription: One or more pixels on the screen are permanently lit (stuck) or permanently dark (dead).
fullDescription:
Stuck and dead pixels are a common issue on LCD and OLED panels. A stuck pixel remains permanently lit in one color (red, green, or blue), while a dead pixel is permanently dark and does not light up at all. These defects are visible as tiny dots on the screen that do not change with the displayed content.

Stuck pixels are caused by a transistor malfunction that keeps the pixel sub-element permanently energized. Dead pixels result from a transistor that has completely failed and no longer drives the pixel. On OLED panels, stuck pixels are less common, but dead sub-pixels can occur.

Most TV manufacturers, including Sony, have a pixel defect policy that allows a certain number of stuck or dead pixels before the panel is considered defective under warranty. Typically, 3-5 dead pixels or a larger cluster is required before warranty replacement is offered. Single stuck pixels can sometimes be fixed through pixel-cycling techniques.

causes.cause: Manufacturing defect in the panel transistor
Transistor failure from age or heat exposure
Physical pressure damage to the panel
Temporary stuck pixel from static image

symptoms: Tiny bright dot visible on dark backgrounds (stuck pixel)
Tiny dark dot visible on bright backgrounds (dead pixel)
Colored dot (red, green, or blue) that does not change
Cluster of abnormal pixels in one area
Pixel defect visible from normal viewing distance

fixSteps.title: Confirm the pixel defect
fixSteps.instruction: Display a solid white, black, red, green, and blue screen on the TV (search 'dead pixel test' on YouTube). Examine the screen carefully from normal viewing distance. Mark the location of any stuck or dead pixels.

fixSteps.title: Run a pixel refresh cycle (OLED models)
fixSteps.instruction: For OLED models, go to Settings > Display & Sound > Expert panel settings > Panel refresh. Run the panel refresh feature. This can sometimes revive stuck pixels on OLED panels. The process takes about an hour.

fixSteps.title: Try pixel cycling videos
fixSteps.instruction: Search YouTube for 'stuck pixel fix' and play a pixel cycling video that rapidly flashes colors. Display it for 30-60 minutes. This can sometimes unstick a stuck pixel by rapidly cycling the transistor. This does not work for dead pixels.

fixSteps.title: Contact Sony for warranty replacement
fixSteps.instruction: If the TV is under warranty and has multiple dead pixels or a prominent stuck pixel visible from normal viewing distance, contact Sony support. Provide photos of the defect and your TV model number and serial number.
tools: Phone or computer for contacting support

partsNeeded.part: Replacement LCD/OLED panel (if out of warranty)

whenToCallPro: Dead pixels cannot be repaired by the end user. If the TV is under warranty and meets Sony's pixel defect policy criteria, Sony will replace the panel. If out of warranty, panel replacement cost is usually not economical — consider whether the defect is visible during normal viewing.
videoSearchQuery: Sony Bravia TV stuck dead pixel fix test

faq.q: How many dead pixels before Sony replaces the TV?
faq.a: Sony's pixel defect policy varies by model and region. Generally, multiple dead pixels (3-5+) or a cluster visible from normal viewing distance qualifies for warranty replacement.

faq.q: Can a stuck pixel fix itself?
faq.a: Stuck pixels can sometimes resolve themselves over time or through pixel cycling techniques. Dead pixels, where the transistor has completely failed, are permanent.

faq.q: Is a single dead pixel normal?
faq.a: A single dead pixel on a panel with millions of pixels is considered within manufacturing tolerance by most TV manufacturers. It is technically a defect but generally not covered under warranty as a standalone issue.
